fa-magic
Welcome to DeployGuard
What DeployGuard is, who uses it, and how it all fits together
intro overview platform welcome

Welcome to DeployGuard Security Services Platform

DeployGuard is a complete management system built specifically for security companies in Namibia. It handles every part of running a security business — from scheduling guards to paying them, billing clients, and tracking everything in between.

Who uses DeployGuard?

  • Operations Managers — Build rosters, review attendance, handle AWOL guards
  • HR / Admin Staff — Manage guard profiles, documents, leave requests
  • Payroll Officers — Process monthly pay runs, review payslips
  • Finance / Billing — Create client invoices, record payments
  • Supervisors — Post daily attendance, raise incidents
  • Company Directors — View the executive dashboard for a live overview

The main areas of the system

AreaWhat it does
Guards & WorkforceGuard profiles, grades, certifications, documents
Roster & SchedulingMonthly roster creation, shift assignments, sites and posts
AttendanceDaily check-in / check-out, AWOL tracking
PayrollMonthly pay runs, payslips, PAYE and SSC calculations
LeaveLeave requests, approvals, leave balances
BillingClient contracts, invoices, payment recording
DisciplineIncidents, warnings, reliability scores
Equipment & FleetUniforms, radios, vehicles, shuttle runs
AI AssistantAsk questions, get insights, navigate with natural language
Tip: Start with the Executive Dashboard. It shows a live summary of everything happening in your company right now — guards active today, AWOL count, pending actions, revenue, and more.
Navigating the System
How to find your way around — menus, breadcrumbs, and quick actions
navigation menu breadcrumb search

Finding Your Way Around

The Top Menu Bar

The top bar runs across every page. From left to right it contains:

  • App icon / Home — click to go back to the main app list
  • Menu name — shows which section you are in (e.g. "Roster & Scheduling")
  • Sub-menus — click to expand the current section's options
  • Search bar — press / anywhere to jump to the search bar
  • Notification bell — alerts about AWOL guards, overdue invoices, expiring documents
  • AI chat button — bottom-right corner, always available

Breadcrumbs

Breadcrumbs appear at the top of record pages, e.g. Roster Batches > June 2026 Roster. Click any part to go back without losing your place.

List vs Form views

  • List view — shows many records at once (e.g. all guards). Click a row to open it.
  • Form view — shows one record in full detail. Use the arrows ‹ › to browse to the next or previous record.
  • Kanban view — card-based layout used for some workflows (e.g. incidents by status).

Searching and Filtering

Every list view has a search bar at the top right. You can:

  • Type a name or keyword and press Enter to filter
  • Click the ▼ Filters dropdown for preset filters (e.g. "AWOL today", "Draft invoices")
  • Click Group By to group records (e.g. group guards by grade)
Remember: Use the AI chat assistant (bottom-right button) to navigate quickly. Just type "show me AWOL guards today" or "open the June payroll" and it will take you there.
Understanding the Executive Dashboard
Reading KPI cards, tabs, charts, and quick-action buttons
dashboard kpi overview executive charts

The Executive Dashboard

The Executive Dashboard is your command centre — a live, real-time view of your entire security operation. Open it from the Security Suite home page or the main menu.

KPI Cards (the big numbers at the top)

The row of coloured cards shows the most important numbers right now:

  • Active Guards — total guards currently employed and active
  • Present Today — guards who checked in for today's shifts
  • AWOL Today — guards who did not show up without notifying
  • Unassigned Slots — open shift slots with no guard assigned yet
  • Pending Leave — leave requests waiting for approval
  • Open Incidents — disciplinary incidents not yet resolved
  • Overdue Invoices — client invoices past their due date
  • Revenue MTD — money invoiced to clients this calendar month

Any number shown in red needs your attention. Click a card to jump to the related records.

The Tabs

Below the KPI cards are tabs that show different views of your data:

  • Overview — status summary, pending actions list, active alerts
  • Roster — today's shift assignments and coverage by site
  • Workforce — guard availability and reliability breakdown
  • Finance — billing summary, payment status, revenue trend

Quick Actions (the grid of buttons)

The button grid at the bottom of the Overview tab is a shortcut to every main module. Click Fleet, Documents, Payslips, etc. to open that section directly.

Best practice: Start every work day on the Executive Dashboard. Check for AWOL guards and unassigned slots first thing in the morning.
Key Concepts Explained
Guards, Posts, Sites, Slots, Batches — what they mean and how they connect
concepts glossary guard post site slot batch roster

Key Concepts in DeployGuard

Before you dive in, it helps to understand how the main building blocks connect.

Guards (Employees)

A guard is any person employed to provide security services. In DeployGuard, each guard has a profile that includes their grade, certifications, reliability score, and document status. Guards are the core of everything.

Client Sites

A client site is a physical location that your company guards — a shopping mall, a bank, a construction site. Each site belongs to a client and can have multiple posts.

Posts

A post is a specific position within a site — for example, "Main Gate" or "Control Room" at a particular mall. Guards are assigned to posts, not just sites.

Shift Templates

A shift template defines the working hours — e.g. "Day Shift: 06:00–18:00" or "Night Shift: 18:00–06:00". Templates are reused across many roster slots.

Shift Requirements

A shift requirement says "Post X needs 2 guards on the Day Shift every weekday". Requirements drive the automatic roster generation.

Roster Batches

A roster batch is a complete month's worth of shifts. When you generate a roster, the system creates one batch (e.g. "June 2026") containing all individual slots for that month.

Roster Slots

A slot is one shift on one day at one post — the smallest unit. Example: "John Nangolo, Main Gate, Day Shift, 5 June 2026". Slots start unassigned and are filled one by one.

Attendance Records

When a guard arrives for their shift, a supervisor records their check-in time. This creates an attendance record that is compared against the roster slot to calculate hours worked, late arrivals, and AWOL status.

The Workflow in One Picture

Client Site → Posts → Shift Requirements
↓
Roster Batch (Generate) → Roster Slots (Assign guards)
↓
Attendance Records (Check-in/out) → Payroll (Calculate pay)
↓
Billing (Invoice client) → Payments (Record receipts)
Adding a New Guard
Step-by-step guide to creating a guard profile from scratch
guard employee add create new profile

Adding a New Guard

Before a guard can be rostered, paid, or tracked, they need a profile in the system.

Step 1 — Open the Employee list

Go to Workforce → Guards (or Employees in the main menu). Click the New button in the top-left.

Step 2 — Fill in basic details

  • Name — full legal name as on ID document
  • Job Position — select "Security Guard" or equivalent
  • Grade — select the guard's grade (A, B, C). Grade determines which posts they can work and their pay rate.
  • Date of Birth and ID Number
  • Phone / Email
  • Bank Account — needed for payroll

Step 3 — Assign certifications

Under the Certifications tab, add any qualifications the guard holds (e.g. First Aid, Firearm Competency). Enter the expiry date where applicable — the system will alert you before it expires.

Step 4 — Upload documents

Under the Documents tab (or via Documents → Employee Documents), upload copies of:

  • National ID or Passport
  • Security licence / PSIRA certificate
  • Firearm competency (if applicable)
  • Medical clearance

Step 5 — Save

Click Save. The guard's profile is now active and can be used in rosters.

Important: A guard cannot be assigned to a post that requires a higher grade than theirs. Make sure you set the correct grade before building the roster.
Guard Grades & Certifications
What grades mean, how they affect pay and post eligibility
grade certification qualification post eligibility pay rate

Guard Grades & Certifications

What is a Grade?

Every guard is assigned a grade (A, B, C, or custom). Grades serve two purposes:

  1. Pay rate — each grade has its own hourly rate used in payroll calculations
  2. Post eligibility — posts can require a minimum grade. A Grade C guard cannot be assigned to a Grade A post.

Setting Up Grades

Go to Configuration → Guard Grades. For each grade, set:

  • Name (e.g. "Grade A")
  • Hourly rate (N$/hour)
  • Description (responsibilities level)

What are Certifications?

Certifications are qualifications a guard must hold to work certain posts. Examples:

  • First Aid Level 1
  • Firearm Competency (Handgun)
  • VIP Protection
  • CCTV Operator

Setting Up Certifications

Go to Configuration → Certifications. For each certification, decide whether it expires (most training certifications need renewal every 1–2 years).

Assigning Certifications to Guards

Open a guard's profile and go to the Certifications tab. Click Add a line to link the certification. If it has an expiry date, the system will send a notification 30 days before it expires.

Post Requirements

Each post type can have required certifications. When the AI shift planner suggests guards for a slot, it will only suggest guards who hold all required certifications and have the correct grade.
Guard Reliability Scores
How reliability scores are calculated and what they mean for rostering
reliability score performance awol attendance discipline

Guard Reliability Scores

The reliability score is a single number (0–100) that summarises how dependable a guard is. It affects rostering priority and is visible on the Roster Board when selecting guards.

What affects the score?

EventImpact
AWOL (no-show)−10 points
Absence (notified)−3 points
Late arrival−2 points
Disciplinary incident (approved)−5 to −20 points (by severity)
Full shift completed on time+1 point (capped)
Manual adjustmentSet by manager (positive or negative)

Score bands

  • 80–100 (Green) — Excellent. Prioritised for critical posts.
  • 60–79 (Amber) — Good. Suitable for most posts.
  • 40–59 (Orange) — Below average. Monitor closely.
  • Below 40 (Red) — Poor. Consider performance review.

Manual Adjustments

Managers can add a manual reliability adjustment from the guard's profile under Configuration → Reliability Adjustments. This is useful for rewarding exceptional performance or correcting a one-off unfair deduction.
Managing Guard Documents
Uploading, verifying, and tracking expiry of guard documents
documents identity licence firearm medical expiry verification

Managing Guard Documents

DeployGuard tracks all compliance documents for each guard and alerts you before they expire.

Document Types

  • Identity — National ID, passport
  • Employment — Employment contract, tax form
  • Training / Certification — Security licence, First Aid, etc.
  • Medical — Fitness certificate, medical clearance
  • Clearance — Police clearance, background check
  • Client — Site-specific access cards or client-required documents

Adding a Document

  1. Go to Workforce → Employee Documents
  2. Click New
  3. Select the guard (Employee), document type, and issue date
  4. Enter the expiry date (if the document expires)
  5. Click the attachment icon to upload a scanned copy
  6. Save — the document status starts as Draft

Verifying a Document

Once you have confirmed the document is genuine, click Verify. The document moves to Verified status and a timestamp is recorded.

Expiry Alerts

The system automatically sends notifications 30 days before a document expires. You will see an alert in the notification bell. Documents transition automatically from Verified → Expiring → Expired based on the expiry date.

Warning: Guards with an expired firearm competency certificate should not be assigned to armed posts. The system will warn you during rostering.
How Rosters Work
The complete lifecycle from draft to confirmed roster
roster lifecycle draft generated submitted approved confirmed

How Rosters Work

DeployGuard uses a structured roster workflow to ensure every shift is properly planned, reviewed, and approved before the month begins.

The Roster Lifecycle

📝 Draft → ⚙️ Generated → 📤 Submitted → ✓ Approved → 🔒 Confirmed

Draft

You create a new roster batch for the month. Nothing is generated yet — this is just the header record (month, year).

Generated

You click Generate Roster and the system creates individual shift slots from your shift requirements. These slots are empty (no guard assigned yet).

Submitted

After assigning guards to all slots, you submit the roster for management review.

Approved

A manager reviews and approves the roster. Guards can now be notified of their shifts.

Confirmed

The roster is locked. No further changes can be made. Payroll can now process attendance against these shifts.

What are Shift Requirements?

Before generating a roster, you need shift requirements — these tell the system how many guards are needed at each post, on which days, and at what times. Set them up under Scheduling → Shift Requirements for each site and post.
Creating a Monthly Roster
Step-by-step: from blank batch to fully assigned roster
roster create generate monthly batch slots assign

Creating a Monthly Roster

Before you start — Prerequisites

Make sure these are set up first:

  • Client sites and posts are created
  • Shift templates exist (e.g. Day 06:00–18:00, Night 18:00–06:00)
  • Shift requirements are configured for each post
  • Guards have correct grades and active documents

Step 1 — Create a new Roster Batch

  1. Go to Scheduling → Roster Batches
  2. Click New
  3. Set the Month and Year
  4. Give it a name (e.g. "June 2026 Roster")
  5. Save — status is now Draft

Step 2 — Generate Slots

  1. Click the Generate Roster button
  2. The system reads all shift requirements and creates one slot per requirement per day
  3. Status moves to Generated
  4. You will now see all empty slots listed under the batch

Step 3 — Assign Guards

You have two ways to assign guards:

  • Roster Board (recommended) — an interactive visual board. Open it from Scheduling → Roster Board. See the Roster Board article for details.
  • Slot list — open each slot individually and select a guard from the dropdown.

Step 4 — Submit for Approval

Once all slots are filled, click Submit on the Roster Batch record. A manager must then click Approve.

Step 5 — Confirm

After approval, click Confirm to lock the roster. It is now ready for attendance posting.

Tip: Use the AI Shift Planner to get ranked guard suggestions for each empty slot. It checks grades, certifications, leave conflicts, and double-bookings automatically.
Using the Roster Board
The interactive visual tool for assigning guards to shifts
roster board assign guard slot visual interactive suggestion

The Roster Board

The Roster Board is an interactive visual tool that makes assigning guards to shifts much faster. Open it from Scheduling → Roster Board.

Layout

  • Left sidebar — Select the month, site, and view options
  • Main grid — Shows the calendar with all slots. Rows = posts, columns = days.
  • Slot cards — Each cell is a shift slot. Colours show assignment status (empty = grey, assigned = blue).

Assigning a Guard

  1. Click on an empty (grey) slot
  2. A panel opens on the right showing Suggested Guards
  3. The AI ranks candidates by score — best match at the top
  4. Each suggestion shows the guard's name, grade, reliability score, and any flags (leave conflict, certification warning)
  5. Click Assign next to a guard to fill the slot

Reading the Suggestion Cards

  • ● Green score — Best available match
  • ● Amber score — Acceptable but some concerns
  • ● Red score — Not recommended (shown for completeness)
  • ⚠ Leave conflict — This guard has approved leave on this date
  • ⚠ Double booked — Guard is already assigned to another slot at the same time

Removing or Swapping an Assignment

Click on a filled (blue) slot to see the current assignment. Click Remove to clear it, then assign a different guard.

Tip: Filter by site in the left sidebar to focus on one client at a time. This is much easier than scrolling through all slots at once.
Handling AWOL Guards
What to do when a guard does not show up for their shift
awol absent no-show replacement slot emergency

Handling AWOL Guards

AWOL (Absent Without Leave) means a guard did not show up for a scheduled shift without prior notice.

Step 1 — Mark the guard AWOL

  1. Go to Attendance → Posting Console (or open the attendance batch for today)
  2. Find the slot for the missing guard
  3. Set the status to AWOL
  4. The system records the time and decrements the guard's reliability score by 10 points

Step 2 — Find a Replacement

  1. Go to Scheduling → Roster Board
  2. Find the affected slot (it will show as unfilled/AWOL)
  3. Click the slot to see replacement suggestions
  4. The AI suggests available guards considering the current time (emergency fill)
  5. Call the guard and assign them to the slot

Step 3 — Raise a Disciplinary Incident

AWOL is a disciplinary matter. You should raise a formal incident:

  1. Go to Discipline → Incidents → New
  2. Select the guard, set type to "AWOL / Absence", attach the roster slot
  3. Save and submit for approval

AWOL Heatmap

The AWOL heatmap (under Attendance → AWOL Heatmap) shows patterns over time — which guards are repeatedly AWOL, and on which days/sites. Use this to spot systemic issues.
Recording Daily Attendance
How to capture check-ins and check-outs using attendance batches
attendance check-in check-out posting batch daily

Recording Daily Attendance

Every day, supervisors must record whether each guard showed up for their shift. This is done through attendance batches.

What is an Attendance Batch?

An attendance batch is a daily sheet that groups all the shift slots for a specific day and site. One batch = one day's attendance for a set of guards.

Creating a Batch

  1. Go to Attendance → Posting Sheets
  2. Click New
  3. Select the Date and Site
  4. The system loads all roster slots for that date/site automatically
  5. Save — the batch starts in Draft status

Capturing Attendance

For each slot in the batch:

  • Enter the actual check-in time
  • Enter the actual check-out time (end of shift)
  • The system automatically calculates: late arrival, early departure, total hours worked
  • Set the status: Present, Late, Absent, or AWOL

Submitting the Batch

Once all guards are captured, click Mark as Captured. A supervisor then reviews and clicks Review. Finally, lock the batch — no changes can be made after locking.

Important: Batches must be locked before payroll runs. Unlocked attendance records are not included in payslip calculations.
The Posting Console
A fast, touch-friendly interface for capturing attendance on the spot
posting console check-in fast tablet mobile attendance

The Posting Console

The Posting Console is a streamlined interface designed for supervisors to quickly record attendance — optimised for tablets and touch screens used at the gate.

Opening the Console

Go to Attendance → Posting Console. Select the date and site from the dropdowns.

How to Use It

  1. The console shows a card for each expected guard shift
  2. As guards arrive, tap their card and enter the check-in time
  3. At the end of a shift, tap again to record the check-out time
  4. Guards not present can be marked AWOL or Absent with a single tap

Status Colours

  • Present — on time and working
  • Late — arrived after scheduled start time
  • AWOL — did not show, no notice given
  • Absent — absent with prior notification

Why Use the Console?

The console is much faster than the standard form view for high-volume check-ins. On a busy morning shift at a large site, you can process 30+ guards in minutes rather than opening individual records.
Attendance Statuses Explained
What Present, Late, Early Leave, Absent, and AWOL mean — and how each affects pay
status present late early leave absent awol pay impact

Attendance Statuses Explained

StatusMeaningPay ImpactReliability Impact
Present On time, completed shift Full hours paid +1 point
Late Arrived after scheduled start time Hours from actual check-in only −2 points
Early Leave Left before scheduled end time Hours up to actual check-out only −2 points
Absent Did not work, notified in advance Zero pay for the shift −3 points
AWOL No show, no notice given Zero pay; may trigger deduction −10 points

Payable Hours

The system calculates payable hours for each record — this is the actual time worked within the scheduled window. Overtime beyond the scheduled window is tracked separately.
How Payroll Works
The end-to-end payroll process from attendance to net pay
payroll overview period payslip gross net process

How Payroll Works

DeployGuard automates the Namibian payroll process, calculating gross pay from attendance, then deducting SSC, PAYE, loans, and discipline deductions to arrive at net pay.

The Payroll Process (in order)

  1. Lock attendance — all posting batches for the period must be locked
  2. Create a Payroll Period — defines the month/period being paid
  3. Process the Period — the system generates payslips for all active guards
  4. Review Payslips — check individual payslips for errors
  5. Confirm Payslips — finalise, ready for payment
  6. Mark as Paid — record that bank transfers were made
  7. Close the Period — lock the period; no further changes

What goes into Gross Pay?

  • Normal hours × hourly rate
  • Saturday hours × 1.25 (Saturday premium)
  • Sunday hours × 1.5 (Sunday premium)
  • Public holiday hours × 1.5
  • Night shift hours × 1.25 (if applicable)
  • Overtime hours × 1.5

What is deducted?

  • SSC — Social Security Commission (employee portion: 0.9% of gross)
  • PAYE — Pay As You Earn income tax (Namibian tax brackets)
  • Loan repayments — active employee loans
  • Discipline deductions — approved incident penalties
  • Equipment damage — costs for damaged/lost equipment
Net Pay Floor: DeployGuard enforces a minimum net pay floor of 30% of gross. If deductions would leave the guard with less than 30%, the deductions are capped.
Running a Payroll Period
Step-by-step guide to processing and finalising monthly payroll
payroll period process run monthly step by step

Running a Payroll Period

Before you start

Confirm all attendance batches for the period are locked. Go to Attendance → Posting Sheets and filter by the month — all should show status "Locked".

Step 1 — Create the Payroll Period

  1. Go to Payroll → Payroll Periods
  2. Click New
  3. Set the Name (e.g. "June 2026"), Start Date, and End Date
  4. Select the Payroll Rule Set (Namibia Standard)
  5. Save — status is Draft

Step 2 — Process the Period

  1. Click Process on the period record
  2. The system calculates gross pay for all guards based on locked attendance
  3. Status moves to Processed
  4. Individual payslips are created — one per active guard

Step 3 — Review Payslips

Click View Payslips to see all generated payslips. Review any that look unusual (very high or very low amounts). Open a payslip to see the full breakdown.

Step 4 — Confirm and Pay

  1. Select all correct payslips and click Confirm
  2. Process bank transfers outside the system
  3. Come back and click Mark as Paid on the period
  4. Click Close Period to lock it permanently
Note: Closing a period cannot be undone. Make sure all payslips are correct before closing.
Understanding a Payslip
How to read the payslip breakdown — earnings, deductions, and net pay
payslip read earnings deductions gross net PAYE SSC understanding

Understanding a Payslip

Each guard receives a payslip every pay period. Here is how to read it.

Earnings Section

Lists every type of pay earned this period:

  • Normal Hours — weekday hours at base rate
  • Saturday Hours — at 1.25× rate
  • Sunday Hours — at 1.50× rate
  • Public Holiday Hours — at 1.50× rate
  • Night Shift Hours — at 1.25× rate
  • Overtime — at 1.50× rate

Gross Pay = sum of all earning lines

Deductions Section

  • SSC Employee — 0.9% of gross (Social Security Commission)
  • PAYE — income tax calculated on annual equivalent earnings
  • Loan Repayment — monthly instalment for any active employee loan
  • Discipline — approved disciplinary deductions
  • Equipment — cost of damaged or lost equipment

Net Pay

Net Pay = Gross Pay − Total Deductions

This is the amount the guard receives in their bank account.

Payslip Statuses

  • Draft — generated but not yet confirmed. Can still be edited.
  • Confirmed — finalised and ready for payment. No further edits.
  • Paid — payment has been made to the guard.
Namibian PAYE & SSC Explained
How income tax and social security contributions are calculated
PAYE SSC tax Namibia income social security contribution bracket

Namibian PAYE & SSC

DeployGuard is configured with Namibian payroll rules. Here is how the two main statutory deductions work.

SSC — Social Security Commission

Both the employee and employer contribute to the Namibian Social Security Commission:

  • Employee contribution: 0.9% of gross monthly earnings
  • Employer contribution: 0.9% of gross monthly earnings (this is a company cost, not deducted from the guard)

The SSC contribution covers maternity leave, sick leave, and death benefits.

PAYE — Pay As You Earn Income Tax

PAYE is calculated using Namibian annual tax brackets applied to the annualised monthly gross:

  1. Monthly gross is multiplied by 12 to get annual equivalent
  2. Tax is calculated on this annual figure using the bracket table
  3. The annual tax is divided by 12 to get the monthly PAYE deduction

Namibian Tax Brackets (approximate)

Annual Income (N$)Tax Rate
0 – 50,0000% (exempt)
50,001 – 100,00018%
100,001 – 300,00025% – 32%
Above 300,00037%

Exact brackets are configurable under Configuration → Payroll Rule Sets → Tax Brackets.
Types of Leave
Annual, sick, compassionate — what each type covers and how it accrues
leave type annual sick compassionate accrual balance

Types of Leave

DeployGuard supports multiple leave types, each with its own accrual policy and rules.

Common Leave Types

TypeWhat it coversTypical allowance
Annual LeavePlanned time off, vacation24 days/year (2 days/month accrual)
Sick LeaveMedical illness or injury30 days per 3-year cycle
CompassionateDeath of immediate family member3–5 days per event
Study LeaveExaminations or trainingVaries by policy

Leave Accrual

Annual leave accrues over time. By default, a guard earns 2 days of leave for every month they work. Accrual only applies when the guard is actively working — not during unpaid leave periods.

Leave Balances

Each guard's current leave balance is shown on their profile under the Leave tab, and in Leave → Leave Balances. The balance shows days available, days taken, and days pending approval.

Year-End

At year-end, the system can:

  • Carry over unused leave (up to a configured cap)
  • Forfeit excess leave above the cap
  • Pay out unused leave (configured per leave type)
Submitting & Approving Leave Requests
How to create a leave request and approve or refuse it
leave request submit approve refuse draft

Leave Requests

Creating a Leave Request

  1. Go to Leave → Leave Requests → New
  2. Select the Guard (employee)
  3. Select the Leave Type (Annual, Sick, etc.)
  4. Enter the Start Date and End Date
  5. Add a reason in the Description field
  6. Save — status is Draft
  7. Click Submit to send it for approval

Approving a Leave Request

  1. Go to Leave → Leave Requests
  2. Filter by Submitted status
  3. Open the request and review the dates
  4. Check the roster — does the guard have shifts on those days? Are there enough other guards to cover?
  5. Click Approve (or Refuse with a reason)

What happens after approval?

  • The guard's leave balance is reduced by the number of approved days
  • The roster board will show a leave conflict warning if someone tries to assign the guard to a shift during their leave period
  • Public holidays within the leave period are not counted as leave days
Tip: The AI Assistant can check roster impact before you approve. Ask it: "If I approve leave for [Guard Name] from 10 June to 14 June, how many slots are affected?"
Setting Up a Client Contract (Billing Plan)
How to create billing plans for recurring, per-shift, and ad-hoc clients
billing plan contract client recurring per-shift invoice setup

Setting Up a Client Contract

A Billing Plan is the contract between DeployGuard and a client — it defines what services are provided and how much to charge.

Billing Modes

  • Recurring — a fixed monthly fee, invoiced automatically each month
  • Per-Shift — charged based on actual shifts worked (calculated from attendance)
  • Ad-hoc — manual invoices as needed, no automatic generation

Creating a Billing Plan

  1. Go to Billing → Billing Plans → New
  2. Select the Client (partner)
  3. Select the Site this plan covers
  4. Choose the Billing Mode
  5. Set Payment Terms (e.g. Net 30 days)
  6. Set VAT rate (default 15% for Namibia)
  7. Add Plan Lines — one line per service or post type, with the rate per unit
  8. Save and Activate the plan

Plan Lines

Each line specifies what is being charged. For a per-shift plan, lines might be:

  • Grade A Guard — N$XX/hour
  • Grade B Guard — N$XX/hour
  • Vehicle — N$XX/trip
Generating & Managing Invoices
How to generate, confirm, send, and track client invoices
invoice generate confirm send billing client payment status

Client Invoices

Generating Invoices

For recurring plans, the system can auto-generate draft invoices at the start of each month via a scheduled job. You can also generate manually:

  1. Go to Billing → Invoices → New
  2. Select the Billing Plan
  3. Set the invoice Date and Period
  4. Add or adjust line items
  5. Save — status is Draft

Invoice Workflow

Draft → Confirmed → Sent → Paid
  • Confirm — locks the invoice amounts. Client can now be sent the invoice.
  • Mark as Sent — records that the invoice was emailed/delivered to the client.
  • Record Payment — when the client pays, record the payment (see next article).

Overdue Invoices

The system automatically flags invoices as overdue when they pass their due date. These appear in red on the Executive Dashboard and trigger a notification alert.
Recording Client Payments
How to log when a client pays an invoice and reconcile balances
payment client invoice reconcile record bank transfer partial

Recording Client Payments

When a Client Pays

  1. Go to Billing → Client Payments → New
  2. Select the Client
  3. Enter the Amount Received and Payment Date
  4. Select the Payment Method (bank transfer, cash, etc.)
  5. Enter the bank reference or deposit slip number
  6. Save

Allocating to Invoices

After recording the payment, link it to the outstanding invoice(s):

  1. Open the payment record
  2. Under Invoice Allocation, click Add a line
  3. Select the invoice being paid and the amount applied
  4. If the payment covers multiple invoices, add multiple allocation lines
  5. Save — the invoice status will update automatically

Partial Payments

If a client pays less than the full amount, the invoice status becomes Partially Paid. The remaining balance shows on the ageing report.

Invoice Ageing Report

Go to Billing → Invoice Ageing to see a full breakdown of what each client owes, grouped by how many days overdue. Use this for collection follow-ups.
Recording a Disciplinary Incident
How to log an incident, set severity, and submit it for approval
incident discipline record severity log submit approval

Recording a Disciplinary Incident

When a guard behaves in a way that violates company policy, a formal incident record must be created. This ensures a proper paper trail and triggers the appropriate consequences.

Step 1 — Create the Incident

  1. Go to Discipline → Incidents → New
  2. Select the Guard (employee)
  3. Select the Incident Type (e.g. AWOL, Insubordination, Theft)
  4. Set the Incident Date
  5. Write a detailed Description of what happened
  6. Attach evidence if available (photos, witness statements)
  7. Save — status is Draft

Step 2 — Set the Severity

Severity determines the automatic consequences:

  • Low — verbal warning, minor reliability deduction
  • Medium — written warning, moderate deduction
  • High — final warning, significant deduction, possible suspension
  • Critical — immediate dismissal consideration, guard may be disqualified from future assignments

Step 3 — Submit for Approval

Click Submit. A manager must review and click Approve before the consequences take effect. Once approved, the reliability score is automatically adjusted and any payroll deduction is created.

Guard Acknowledgment

The system tracks whether the guard has been shown and acknowledged the incident. Tick the Guard Acknowledged checkbox once the incident has been communicated to them.
The Incident Appeal Process
How guards can appeal an incident and how managers handle appeals
appeal incident overturn upheld discipline fairness

The Incident Appeal Process

DeployGuard supports a formal appeal process for incidents, ensuring fairness and allowing guards to contest decisions they believe are unjust.

Filing an Appeal

  1. Open the approved incident record
  2. Click File Appeal
  3. Enter the guard's grounds for appeal and supporting information
  4. Save — the incident status moves to Under Appeal

Reviewing an Appeal

A senior manager reviews the appeal and decides:

  • Uphold the incident — the original decision stands; consequences remain
  • Overturn the incident — the incident is reversed; reliability score is restored and any deductions are cancelled

After the Decision

The outcome is recorded on the incident with a date and the deciding manager's name. The guard should be informed of the outcome in writing.

Best practice: Always document appeals promptly. A guard should receive a response within 5 working days of filing an appeal.
Issuing Equipment to Guards
How to allocate uniforms, radios, and firearms to a guard
equipment issue allocate uniform radio firearm guard

Issuing Equipment to Guards

DeployGuard tracks all equipment issued to guards — from uniforms to radios and firearms. This creates accountability and enables automatic deductions if items are lost or damaged.

Equipment Types

  • Non-serialised (e.g. uniforms, boots) — tracked by quantity, not individual item
  • Serialised (e.g. radios, firearms) — each item has a unique serial number

Creating an Allocation

  1. Go to Equipment → Allocations → New
  2. Select the Guard
  3. Select the Equipment Type
  4. For serialised items, select the specific Item (serial number)
  5. Enter the quantity (for non-serialised)
  6. Set the Issue Date
  7. Save — status is Allocated
  8. Click Issue to confirm physical handover — status moves to Issued
  9. Once the guard signs for it, click Acknowledge

Returning Equipment

When a guard returns equipment (e.g. end of employment), open the allocation and click Return. Enter the return date and condition of the item.

Equipment Damage

If an item is returned damaged or reported lost, record a damage report on the allocation. The cost is automatically calculated and a payroll deduction is created for the next payslip.
Managing the Vehicle Fleet
Vehicles, shuttle routes, runs, inspections, and fuel logs
fleet vehicle shuttle route run inspection fuel service odometer

Managing the Vehicle Fleet

The Fleet module tracks all company vehicles used for transporting guards, including shuttle runs, inspections, fuel costs, and service history.

Vehicles

Go to Fleet → Vehicles. Each vehicle record includes:

  • Registration number, make, model, year
  • Current odometer reading
  • Assigned driver
  • Status: Available / In Transit / In Service / Scrapped

Shuttle Routes

A shuttle route defines a regular transport path with ordered stops (pickup and drop-off points). Set up routes under Fleet → Routes.

Shuttle Runs

Each actual trip is recorded as a shuttle run. A run records:

  • Which vehicle and driver
  • Which route was followed
  • Which guards boarded, got dropped off, or were no-shows
  • Departure and arrival times

Pre-Departure Inspections

Before each trip, the driver should complete a vehicle inspection. The inspection checklist covers brakes, tyres, lights, fuel level, and more. A failed inspection blocks the vehicle from departing until the issue is resolved.

Fuel Logs

Record every fuel fill-up under Fleet → Fuel Logs. The system tracks consumption (km/litre) per vehicle over time.

Service Records

Log maintenance and service events under Fleet → Service Records. This creates a full service history for each vehicle.
What the AI Assistant Can Do
An overview of AI features and the chat assistant
AI assistant overview features chat gemini claude

The DeployGuard AI Assistant

DeployGuard includes a built-in AI assistant powered by Google Gemini (or Claude/OpenAI depending on your configuration). It can answer questions, find records, and even take actions — after your confirmation.

What can you ask?

  • "How many guards were AWOL this week?"
  • "Show me all overdue invoices"
  • "Who are the top 5 guards by reliability score?"
  • "What shifts are unassigned for next Monday?"
  • "Summarise today's attendance"
  • "Show me the payslip for John Nangolo in June"

What the AI can do for you

  • Answer questions — searches live data and gives you accurate, current numbers
  • Navigate — produces clickable links to records and filtered list views
  • Propose actions — can suggest updating a record or running an action, but always shows a confirmation dialog before doing anything

What the AI cannot do

  • It cannot make changes without your explicit confirmation
  • It cannot access data outside the DeployGuard platform
  • It cannot run payroll or confirm rosters automatically
Privacy: The AI only uses data within your DeployGuard instance. It does not send your guard or client data to external services beyond the AI provider (Gemini/Claude) for generating responses.
Using the Chat Assistant
How to open, use, and get the best results from the AI chat panel
chat assistant panel open message quick reply navigate

Using the AI Chat Panel

Opening the Chat

The chat button is in the bottom-right corner of every page — it looks like a speech bubble icon with the DeployGuard shield. Click it to open the panel. Click again (or the × button) to close it.

Sending a Message

  1. Click in the text box at the bottom of the panel
  2. Type your question or request
  3. Press Enter to send (or Shift+Enter for a new line)
  4. Wait a moment — the AI calls live data tools before answering

Quick Replies

After the AI responds, it often shows suggested follow-up questions as clickable buttons. These are quick replies — tap one to continue the conversation without typing.

Clickable Links in Responses

The AI often includes navigation chips (blue buttons that say "Open Record →" or "View all AWOL →"). Click these to jump directly to the relevant page.

Action Confirmation

If you ask the AI to do something (e.g. "Approve this leave request"), it will show a confirmation card describing exactly what it plans to do. You must click Confirm for the action to execute. You can always click Cancel to decline.

Starting a New Chat

Click the + icon in the chat header to clear the conversation and start fresh. Previous conversations are saved to session history.

Tips for Better Responses

  • Be specific: "AWOL guards this week" is better than "attendance problems"
  • Include names or dates when relevant: "payslip for John Nangolo June 2026"
  • Ask one question at a time for clearer answers
Setting Up the AI Engine
How administrators configure the AI provider and API key
AI setup configuration API key Gemini Claude provider admin

Setting Up the AI Engine

Before the AI chat assistant works, an administrator must configure the AI provider and enter an API key.

Step 1 — Open AI Configuration

Go to Configuration → AI Engine (you must be an administrator).

Step 2 — Choose a Provider

Select your preferred AI provider:

  • Google Gemini — recommended. Requires a Gemini API key from Google AI Studio.
  • Anthropic Claude — high quality. Requires an API key from console.anthropic.com.
  • OpenAI — GPT-4 based. Requires an API key from platform.openai.com.

Step 3 — Enter the API Key

Paste your API key into the corresponding field (e.g. Gemini API Key). Click Save.

Step 4 — Test the Connection

Click Test Connection. If the key is valid, you will see a success message. If not, check that the key is correct and has not expired.

Optional Settings

  • Max Tokens — maximum length of AI responses (default: 4096, recommended for complex queries)
  • Temperature — 0.1–0.3 for factual answers, higher for creative text (default: 0.2)
  • Response Cache — reuses identical query responses for 24 hours, reducing API costs
Cost awareness: Each question sent to the AI uses a small amount of API tokens. Enabling the response cache significantly reduces costs for repeated questions.
Zambia Payroll
fa-flag
ZM
ZM
Setting Up Zambia Payroll
How to create and configure a Zambian payroll rule set
zambia payroll setup rule set ZMW configuration NAPSA NHIMA PAYE

Setting Up Zambia Payroll

DogForce includes a Zambia-specific payroll localization that handles NAPSA, NHIMA, WCF, and PAYE automatically. Before processing your first Zambian payroll, you need a correctly configured rule set.

Step 1 — Open Payroll Rule Sets

Go to Payroll → Configuration → Payroll Rule Sets.

Step 2 — Find or Create the Zambia Rule Set

A default Zambia Default Rule Set is pre-installed. Open it to review the settings. If you need a separate rule set (e.g. for different company entities), click New and set the Country Code to ZM.

Step 3 — Configure Zambia Statutory Deductions

In the rule set form, find the Zambia Statutory Deductions section:

FieldDefaultNotes
Employee NAPSA Rate5%Standard statutory rate
Employer NAPSA Rate5%Company cost, not deducted from guard
NAPSA Monthly Earnings CeilingZMW 34,164Update annually from NAPSA gazette
Employee NHIMA Rate0.5%No earnings ceiling applies
Employer NHIMA Rate0.5%Company cost
Employer WCF Rate1.75%Confirm your actual rate via WCFCB portal
PAYE Tax Brackets2025 ZRA bracketsSelect the correct year's bracket set

Step 4 — Set the Currency to ZMW

On the rule set form, confirm the Currency is set to ZMW (Zambian Kwacha). All payslips for this rule set will display amounts in ZMW.

Step 5 — Create a Payroll Period

Go to Payroll → Payroll Periods → New and link the period to your Zambia rule set. The system will then apply ZM statutory deductions automatically when payslips are processed.

Annual Update Reminder: At the start of each year, check NAPSA's gazette notice for the new earnings ceiling, and the ZRA Budget bulletin for any PAYE bracket changes. Update these in the rule set before the January payroll run.
ZM
NAPSA Contributions Explained
National Pension Scheme Authority — rates, ceiling, and how it appears on payslips
NAPSA pension contribution 5% ceiling cap ZMW employee employer

NAPSA — National Pension Scheme Authority

NAPSA is Zambia's mandatory national pension fund, governed by the NAPSA Act Cap 256. Both the employee and employer contribute each month.

Contribution Rates

ContributorRatePaid by
Employee5% of monthly gross earningsDeducted from guard's pay
Employer5% of monthly gross earningsCompany cost — not on the payslip deductions

Monthly Earnings Ceiling

NAPSA contributions are capped — both employee and employer contributions are calculated on a maximum of ZMW 34,164 per month (2025 ceiling). If a guard earns more than this, the contribution is calculated only on ZMW 34,164.

Example — Guard earning ZMW 20,000/month:
Employee NAPSA = 20,000 × 5% = ZMW 1,000

Example — Guard earning ZMW 40,000/month (above ceiling):
Employee NAPSA = 34,164 × 5% = ZMW 1,708.20

NAPSA and PAYE

Under the Zambia Income Tax Act Cap 323, NAPSA contributions are tax-deductible. The system automatically deducts NAPSA before calculating PAYE, reducing the taxable income. This is handled automatically — no manual adjustment needed.

NAPSA Member Number on Guard Profiles

Each guard who has a NAPSA number should have it recorded. Go to Workforce → Guards, open the guard's profile, and find the Zambian Statutory Registration section. Enter the NAPSA Member Number in the field provided. This number appears on the ZM payslip.

Annual Update: NAPSA publishes a new monthly earnings ceiling each year via gazette notice. Update this value in the Payroll Rule Set before January's payroll run.
ZM
NHIMA Levy Explained
National Health Insurance Management Authority — 0.5% levy, no ceiling
NHIMA health insurance levy 0.5% employee employer no ceiling

NHIMA — National Health Insurance Management Authority

NHIMA is Zambia's national health insurance scheme, established by NHIMA Act No. 2 of 2018. Like NAPSA, both the employee and employer contribute.

Contribution Rates

ContributorRateCeiling
Employee0.5% of monthly grossNone — applied to full gross
Employer0.5% of monthly grossNone — applied to full gross

Key Difference from NAPSA

Unlike NAPSA, NHIMA has no monthly earnings ceiling. The 0.5% levy is applied to the guard's full gross pay regardless of how much they earn.

NHIMA and PAYE

NHIMA contributions are not tax-deductible (unlike NAPSA). PAYE is calculated on gross minus NAPSA only — NHIMA does not reduce taxable income.

NHIMA Member Number

Record each guard's NHIMA member number in their profile under Zambian Statutory Registration → NHIMA Member Number. Format is typically NH-XXXXXXX. This number appears on the guard's ZM payslip.

Example — Guard earning ZMW 8,000/month:
Employee NHIMA = 8,000 × 0.5% = ZMW 40.00

Example — Guard earning ZMW 45,000/month:
Employee NHIMA = 45,000 × 0.5% = ZMW 225.00
ZM
Zambia PAYE (Pay As You Earn)
How PAYE is calculated — annual brackets, NAPSA deductibility, and TPIN
PAYE income tax bracket annual ZMW NAPSA deductible taxable TPIN ZRA

Zambia PAYE — Pay As You Earn

PAYE is withheld monthly and remitted to the Zambia Revenue Authority (ZRA). DogForce calculates PAYE automatically when a Zambian payroll is processed.

How PAYE is Calculated

  1. Start with monthly gross pay
  2. Deduct NAPSA — NAPSA is tax-deductible under the Income Tax Act Cap 323. Monthly NAPSA is subtracted from gross to get taxable income.
  3. Annualise — multiply monthly taxable income by 12 to get the annual equivalent
  4. Apply tax brackets — look up the annual figure in the ZRA bracket table to find annual tax
  5. Monthly PAYE — divide annual tax by 12

2025 PAYE Brackets (ZMW)

Annual Taxable IncomeRateFixed Amount
0 – 61,2000% (exempt)—
61,201 – 85,20020%ZMW 0
85,201 – 110,40030%ZMW 4,800
Above 110,40037%ZMW 12,360

Thresholds are for annual taxable income. Source: ZRA Budget 2025. Update annually under Configuration → Payroll Rule Sets → Tax Brackets.

Worked Example

Monthly gross = ZMW 9,000
NAPSA = min(9,000, 34,164) × 5% = ZMW 450
Monthly taxable = 9,000 − 450 = ZMW 8,550
Annual taxable = 8,550 × 12 = ZMW 102,600
Bracket: 85,201–110,400 → fixed ZMW 4,800 + (102,600 − 85,200) × 30%
Annual PAYE = 4,800 + 5,220 = ZMW 10,020
Monthly PAYE = 10,020 ÷ 12 = ZMW 835.00

TPIN Requirement

Each guard should have their TPIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) recorded in their profile under Zambian Statutory Registration → TPIN. The TPIN appears on the payslip and is required for ZRA compliance.
ZM
WCF — Workers' Compensation Fund
Employer-only WCFCB levy — Class IX risk classification, 1.25–2.00%, no ceiling
WCF WCFCB workers compensation employer levy class IX risk rate eWorkers

WCF — Workers' Compensation Fund

The Workers' Compensation Fund Control Board (WCFCB) levy is an employer-only contribution under the Workers' Compensation Act Cap 271. Unlike NAPSA and NHIMA, it is not deducted from the guard's pay — it is a company cost.

Who Sets Your Rate?

The WCFCB assigns each employer a rate based on their specific operational risk profile. Private security companies typically fall under Class IX (Miscellaneous/Services).

Rate Ranges for Private Security

Operations ProfileRate Range
Predominantly unarmed static guarding1.25% – 1.50%
Mixed operations (static + patrol)1.50% – 1.75%
Armed operations, cash-in-transit, or high-risk sites1.75% – 2.00%

The default in the system is 1.75% — suitable for mixed/armed operations. Confirm your exact rate with WCFCB.

Assessable Earnings

WCF is levied on total gross payroll including basic salary, allowances, overtime, and bonuses. There is no earnings ceiling — unlike NAPSA, WCF applies to the full gross.

WCF Report

DogForce can generate a WCF Employer Levy Report per payroll period. Go to Payroll → Reports → WCF Employer Levy and select the period. The report shows each guard's gross pay, assessable earnings, and the employer WCF levy amount — ready for WCFCB submission.

Annual Update Process

  1. Log in to the WCFCB eWorkers Portal at eworkers.wcf.org.zm
  2. Submit your annual wage returns
  3. Confirm your new assessment rate for the coming year
  4. Update the Employer WCF Rate field in your Payroll Rule Set
Reminder: WCF returns and payments are typically due annually. Late submissions attract penalties. Set a reminder to file by the WCFCB deadline each year.
ZM
Reading a Zambian Payslip
How to interpret the ZMW payslip — NAPSA, NHIMA, PAYE entries and net pay
payslip ZMW NAPSA NHIMA PAYE read understand ZM zambia kwacha

Reading a Zambian Payslip

Zambian payslips differ from Namibian ones — they show ZMW amounts and different statutory deductions. Here is how to read each section.

Header Information

  • Company address — shows your company city and country (e.g. Lusaka, Zambia)
  • NAPSA Member No. — guard's NAPSA registration number
  • NHIMA Member No. — guard's NHIMA registration number
  • TPIN — guard's Taxpayer Identification Number from ZRA

Earnings Section (ZMW)

All amounts are in Zambian Kwacha (ZMW). Earnings include:

  • Normal Hours at base hourly rate
  • Saturday/Sunday/Public Holiday premiums
  • Night shift allowance (if applicable)
  • Overtime

Gross Pay = total of all earning lines

Deductions Section (ZMW)

  • NAPSA Contribution — 5% of gross, capped at 5% of ZMW 34,164
  • NHIMA Levy — 0.5% of full gross (no ceiling)
  • Pay As You Earn (PAYE) — income tax, calculated on gross minus NAPSA
  • Loan Repayment (if applicable)
  • Discipline (if applicable)

Net Take-Home Pay

Net Pay (ZMW) = Gross Pay − Total Deductions

The system enforces a 30% net pay floor — if statutory deductions and other deductions would leave the guard with less than 30% of their gross, non-statutory deductions (loans, discipline) are capped first to protect take-home pay.

Why no SSC line? Namibia uses SSC (Social Security Commission). Zambia uses NAPSA and NHIMA instead. The system automatically applies the correct statutory scheme based on the payroll rule set's country code.
ZRA Smart Invoice
fa-qrcode
ZM
ZM
What is ZRA Smart Invoice?
Overview of ZRA's electronic fiscal invoicing requirement for Zambian businesses
ZRA smart invoice fiscal VSDC electronic Zambia requirement

ZRA Smart Invoice

The Zambia Revenue Authority (ZRA) Smart Invoice system is a mandatory electronic fiscal invoicing requirement for registered VAT taxpayers in Zambia. Every invoice issued to a client must be submitted to ZRA's VSDC (Virtual Sales Data Controller) servers and receive a fiscal receipt number before it can be considered a valid tax invoice.

Why is it Required?

ZRA's Smart Invoice system is part of Zambia's digital tax compliance initiative. It ensures:

  • Real-time reporting of all sales transactions to ZRA
  • Unique fiscal receipt numbers on every invoice
  • QR codes on invoices for client verification
  • A tamper-evident audit trail

How DogForce Integrates

DogForce connects directly to the ZRA VSDC API. When you click Submit to ZRA on a confirmed invoice, the system:

  1. Formats the invoice data according to ZRA specifications
  2. Sends it to the VSDC server over a secure HTTPS connection
  3. Receives the fiscal signature, receipt number, and internal data
  4. Records the ZRA receipt number on the invoice
  5. The invoice is now a valid fiscal document

Sandbox vs Production

ZRA provides a Sandbox environment for testing. Test all your integrations in sandbox first before switching to production. See the Configuration article for how to set this up.

Legal Requirement: Failure to submit invoices to ZRA is a compliance violation. Ensure all confirmed invoices are submitted promptly. DogForce will automatically retry failed submissions up to 5 times.
ZM
Configuring the ZRA Connection
How to enter your TPIN, Branch ID, device serial, and choose sandbox or production
ZRA config TPIN BHF ID device serial sandbox production VSDC URL setup

Configuring the ZRA Connection

Before you can submit invoices to ZRA, an administrator must set up the VSDC connection. You will need your ZRA onboarding credentials.

Step 1 — Open ZRA Configuration

Go to ZRA Smart Invoice → Configuration. You need to be a system administrator.

Step 2 — Create a Configuration Record

Click New. Fill in the following fields:

FieldDescriptionExample
NameLabel for this configDogForce ZRA Production
CompanyYour Zambian companyDogForce Security (ZM)
EnvironmentSandbox or ProductionStart with Sandbox
Base URLVSDC server addresshttps://sandbox.zra.org.zm:38083/sandboxvsdc
TPINYour company's 10-digit TPIN1234567890
Branch ID (BHF ID)Branch code from ZRA onboarding000
Device Serial No.VSDC device serial from ZRAProvided in your onboarding letter

Step 3 — Test the Connection

Click the Test Connection button. If successful, you will see a confirmation message. If it fails, check:

  • Is the TPIN correct (exactly 10 digits)?
  • Is the Base URL correct for your environment (sandbox vs production)?
  • Does your server have outbound internet access to the ZRA VSDC endpoint?

Step 4 — Switch to Production

Once you have tested in sandbox and are ready to go live:

  1. Change the Environment field to Production
  2. Update the Base URL to the production VSDC address (provided by ZRA)
  3. Click Test Connection again to confirm
  4. A red warning banner will appear on the config form reminding you this is live
Important: Never use production credentials in the sandbox environment or vice versa. Submitting test invoices to production will create real fiscal records at ZRA.
ZM
Submitting an Invoice to ZRA
Step-by-step: confirming, submitting, and reading the ZRA receipt number
ZRA submit invoice receipt number accepted TPIN button fiscal

Submitting an Invoice to ZRA

Before You Submit

Make sure the following are in place:

  • The invoice is in Confirmed status (not Draft)
  • The customer has a TPIN set on their contact record (required by ZRA for a valid fiscal invoice)
  • A ZRA config record exists and has been tested successfully
Missing Customer TPIN? If the customer has no TPIN, a blue warning banner will appear on the invoice. Open the customer record and enter their TPIN in the Company Tax ID (VAT) field before submitting.

Step 1 — Open the Invoice

Go to Billing → Invoices and open the invoice you want to submit.

Step 2 — Click "Submit to ZRA"

In the header, click the Submit to ZRA button (green, primary). The system connects to the ZRA VSDC server and submits the invoice data.

Step 3 — Check the Result

One of two things will happen:

  • Success (green banner) — the invoice is fiscally accepted. A ZRA Receipt Number is shown on the invoice. The ZRA Accepted banner appears with the receipt number.
  • Error (amber/red banner) — the submission failed. Click ZRA Submissions to see the error message. Fix the issue and retry.

Bulk Submission

To submit multiple invoices at once, go to ZRA Smart Invoice → Bulk Submit. Select the invoices and click Submit All. The result log shows success/failure per invoice.

Automatic Retry

If a submission fails due to a temporary network issue, the system automatically retries up to 5 times using exponential backoff (waiting 2, 10, 30, 120, and 240 minutes between attempts). You do not need to manually retry transient failures.
ZM
Submitting Credit Notes to ZRA
How to fiscally submit a credit note after a ZRA-accepted invoice is reversed
ZRA credit note submit fiscal reverse refund CN

Submitting Credit Notes to ZRA

When you issue a credit note against a ZRA-accepted invoice, the credit note must also be submitted to ZRA as a fiscal credit note. This closes the loop in ZRA's records.

When to Submit a Credit Note

  • A client was overcharged and a refund is being processed
  • Services were not rendered and the invoice needs to be reversed
  • A billing error was discovered after the invoice was fiscally signed

Step 1 — Create the Credit Note

Go to Billing → Credit Notes → New. Link it to the original invoice. Confirm the credit note.

Step 2 — Submit to ZRA

On the confirmed credit note, click Submit Credit Note to ZRA. The system sends the credit note data to the VSDC server, referencing the original invoice's receipt number.

Step 3 — Confirm Acceptance

Once accepted, the credit note shows a ZRA Accepted status. The submission record is visible under ZRA Smart Invoice → Submission Log.

Cancelling a ZRA-Accepted Invoice

If you cancel an invoice that was already accepted by ZRA, the system automatically sends a cancellation request to ZRA (salesStsCd = 05) before cancelling the Odoo record. This keeps ZRA's records in sync. Note: cancellations are logged but do not block the Odoo cancellation if the ZRA request fails.
ZM
Reading the ZRA Submission Log
How to find, read, and act on entries in the ZRA submission log
ZRA submission log state accepted rejected error pending retry

The ZRA Submission Log

Every submission attempt — successful or not — is recorded in the ZRA Submission Log. Go to ZRA Smart Invoice → Submission Log.

Submission States

StateMeaningAction
AcceptedZRA confirmed the invoice is fiscally signedNo action needed
PendingSubmission in progress or awaiting retryWait — system retries automatically
ErrorTechnical failure (network, timeout)System retries; check after 5 retries
RejectedZRA returned a business rejectionFix the underlying data issue (see error message)

Reading a Submission Record

Open any submission to see:

  • ZRA Receipt No. — the fiscal receipt number (only for accepted submissions)
  • ZRA Signature — cryptographic signature from ZRA
  • Error Message — details of why a submission failed
  • Request / Response tabs — the raw JSON sent to and received from ZRA
  • Retry Count — how many times the system has attempted this submission

What to Do After 5 Failed Retries

After 5 automatic retry attempts, the system stops retrying. At this point:

  1. Open the failed submission and read the Error Message
  2. Common causes: expired TPIN, ZRA VSDC server maintenance, invalid invoice data
  3. Fix the root cause
  4. Open the linked invoice and click Submit to ZRA again to create a fresh submission
WhatsApp AI Bridge & Field Attendance Guide Complete guide to supervisors taking field attendance, tracking lateness, bulk site check-ins, and executive stats via WhatsApp. 10 whatsapp ai bridge field attendance lateness site bulk owner stats manager stats awol DeployGuard WhatsApp AI Bridge & Field Operations

DeployGuard connects real-time field operations with Odoo using a zero-cost WhatsApp transport bridge. Supervisors in the field can log attendance, record lateness, mark bulk site check-ins, and query live executive metrics directly over WhatsApp messages.

1. Field Attendance & Lateness Tracking

Supervisors can record guard check-ins directly from site posts:

  • On-time Check-in: Type Check in [Guard Name]. The guard is marked present at their scheduled shift start time.
  • Late Check-in: Type Check in [Guard Name] late [X] minutes (e.g., Check in Winston Zulu late 20 minutes). The system automatically calculates the check-in time variance and logs lateness minutes for payroll.

2. Bulk Site-Level Attendance

Instead of logging individual guards one by one, supervisors can report whole site postings at once:

  • All Present: Type [Site Name] all present (e.g., Bank of Zambia all present). All guards scheduled at the site today are marked present on-time.
  • All Present Except Exceptions: Type [Site Name] all present except [Guard Name] (e.g., Bank of Zambia all present except Winston Zulu). Named guards are flagged as AWOL, while all other site guards are marked present.

3. Flagging Unexcused Absences (AWOL)

To flag an absent guard, type AWOL [Guard Name]. The system logs an unexcused absence, notifies the Central Control Room, and registers an incident on the Central Intelligence Bus.

4. Role-Based Intelligence Commands

  • Owner / Executive Stats (OWNER STATS): Displays active client sites, estimated monthly recurring billing, active posts today, roster coverage rate %, and SLA penalty risks.
  • Manager / Operations Stats (MANAGER STATS): Displays total posts scheduled today, active guards on duty, AWOL gaps, pending check-ins, and top site coverage breakdown.
  • Roster Status (STATUS): Queries daily scheduled posts, active guards, and missing gaps.
  • Command Help (HELP): Displays the interactive WhatsApp command menu.

5. Hybrid Deterministic & AI Processing Architecture

The bridge operates using a two-tier hybrid architecture:

  1. Rule-Based Fast Engine: High-speed regex rule parsing executes instantly without external AI dependencies or costs.
  2. Extensive AI Fallback: If unstructured natural language is received, the message is routed to the security.ai.engine LLM parser to extract intent and execute actions.
